title: Indexes, Query Plans, Operator Costs, and Responsible Tuning · Result-Grain Case: Composite Covering Indexes
summary: A growing ticket dataset makes queue and analytics queries slow, but each new index adds write and storage cost. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql composite covering indexes increment in an evidence-backed query performance dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
Explain B-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-composite-covering-indexes — Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status = 'open' AND severity IN ('high', 'critical')
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tickets_queue
  ON tickets(status, severity, opened_at, ticket_id);
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
